name: F-16 class (nominal Stevens & Lewis / benchmark lineage)
references: Stevens, B. L., Lewis, F. L., & Johnson, E. N. (2015). Aircraft Control and Simulation, 3rd ed. Wiley.
Heidlauf, P., et al. (2018). Verification Challenges in F-16 Ground Collision Avoidance. ARCH@ADHS.
Open benchmark implementations: AeroBenchVVPython, CSAF F-16 (used for cross-checking dynamical structure, not a government release).
notes: Geometry, inertia, and damping-style derivatives follow the Stevens & Lewis / F-16 verification model lineage cited above. CL0 was adjusted slightly from some published tables so wings-level trim at 213 m/s and 5 km matches the same nonlinear EOM used in simulation (residual <1e-8 on udot,wdot,qdot); this is standard when mixing a subset of coefficients into a reduced-order checkpoint model.
